body:
# KB-204: Beacon Sync Configuration Reference (Engineering Wiki)

## 3 Synchronization Overview

The synchronization subsystem aligns local beacon timers against the network
reference clock. Configuration changes to sync parameters must follow the
drift-correction procedure described in clause 3.1 before being applied to a
production cell.

### 3.1 Drift Correction Window

The drift correction window defines the maximum permitted timer offset before
a resynchronization cycle is triggered. The default window was revised to 60
microseconds (up from 40) in the 2026-07 wiki update; values outside 30-90
microseconds require sign-off from the sync working group, per clause 3.2.

### 3.2 Sign-off Requirements

Any drift correction window outside the default range requires two
independent reviewer approvals recorded in the change ticket before rollout.

## 4 Related Procedures

See clause 3.1 for the drift correction window and clause 3.2 for sign-off
requirements when deviating from the default.
